title: Louvre President Christophe Leribault: 'I am not here to undo the work of my predecessors'
subhead: In an interview, the successor to Laurence des Cars as head of the museum explains how he intends to make his mark with humility, courage and ambition.

title: Trump says pausing 'Project Freedom' Hormuz operation in push for Iran deal
Zelensky condemns Russia's deadly strikes in Ukraine as Moscow seeks peace for May 9 parade
'With the withdrawal of American troops from Germany, Berlin is facing a historic dilemma'
subhead: Donald Trump's announcement of the withdrawal of US troops is forcing Berlin to confront the long-standing taboo of its own defense, explains the specialist in Franco-German relations Paul Maurice for Le Monde.

title: Chinese artist Gao Zhen and his family stuck in repressive legal limbo over his Mao sculptures
subhead: Authorities are accusing the visual artist, a US resident, over works created in the 2000s that explicitly criticized Chairman Mao's regime. His wife holds out faint hope that Donald Trump's upcoming visit to Beijing, on May 14 and 15, can help her husband's case.

title: Arsenal's 20-year wait to reach Champions League final
subhead: Bukayo Saka sealed a 1-0 win against Atlético Madrid on Tuesday to bring Mikel Arteta's team to the Champions League for the first time in 20 years. Arsenal will now face Paris Saint-Germain or Bayern Munich in the final on May 30 in Budapest.

title: Military personnel at French presidency suspected of compromising classified documents
subhead: Prosecutors are investigating several members of the military and civilians suspected of setting up a private company to sell classified information and weapons of war. Six suspects have been charged.

Jean-Luc Mélenchon announces fourth presidential run
subhead: Despite the emergence of several figures within La France Insoumise and his promise to step aside after the 2022 election, in which he gathered 21.95% of the vote, the populist leader announced his candidacy on Sunday.

title: Saint Pierre and Miquelon, a pioneering example of climate change adaptation
subhead: This year, residents of a town in the French overseas territory off the coast of Canada are set to move into a new village. This relocation, France's first planned effort to move an entire town, is the result of a lengthy process.

title: Sarkozy's counter-offensive leaves judges unconvinced at Libyan funding trial
subhead: The former French president once again tried on Monday to prove that the sums paid by Libyans were not used to fund his 2007 presidential campaign.
